reviews.review_msg: Dr. Timcke is often regarded as a misunderstood lecturer however, do what is asked such as reading and making use of office hours and you will be fine.

Dr. Scott Timcke is a GREAT lecturer. I am a student in is Communication Lecture and he is amazing! He gives great feedback. A lot of the students think that his lectures are confusing and he is a tough grader but this is simply not true. If you don't attend his lectures, how do you expect to understand what he is teaching? Excellent Lecturer!
Confusing lectures where I feel lost after every class Makes us feel that the standard he set is too high as half of the class failed the mid term

Professor Scott Timecke is smart and sophisticated in his course. He is too smart, which makes the students to have a bit distance from him. Also, he is a bit rude in his way of communication. Perhaps, some humility is needed to achieve full potentials.

At first it may seem like you're doomed for the rest of the semester but Scott isn't really all that bad. He always has good inputs on your assignments, but can also give you the honest, brutal truth of what else is lacking in your work.

Literally the worst teacher I have ever had in my 4 years at SFU.  I had to 'unlearn' how to write a good paper. a paper with a thesis is a bad thing for him.  I hope you like negative feedback, because every time he hands back an assignment he spends half an hour of class time tell everyone how terrible they ALL were! Good Riddance.

Scott's course was a challenge, no doubt, but it was equally rewarding. Scott clearly really cares about his students' learning (he stated this explicitly multiple times), and to that end he let us write multiple drafts of our papers before receiving a final grade on them - I wish every class allowed this.

I learned from Scott while he was a TA, during then there was quite the buzz of him being difficult. unluckily (or luckily) I didn't hear about this until the semester started. He's difficult but he's helpful if you visit his office hours and actually do what you're supposed to do. READ! He's clear on what he wants and is actually very reasonable .

He expects you to read A LOT (not specifically courseware), and looks down at you when you don't read. He is rude to his students, says our writing is sh!t because we don't read but doesn't explain himself. Lectures long and boring, and talks sh!t about other professors. Only took it cause it was 6 credits

Scott was definitely very intimidating at first. His lectures are hard to follow, mainly because of his accent and language. But once you talk to him in person, you start to understand exactly what he's looking for, and how you can excel in his class. Highly recommend for people who are looking to LEARN.
